completion_criteria_checked: schemas/request.schema.json confirmed sound for a contract-foundation task: closed top-level shape (additionalProperties: false), sensible required core fields, request_type and status as closed enums rather than open strings. Minor looseness noted (created_at not date-time-formatted, request_id/source unconstrained strings, payload unconstrained) but judged non-blocking since the lifecycle doc explicitly defers producer/consumer specifics to later tasks.

Sequencing (defining the contract before any producer/consumer exists) confirmed reasonable, not premature, given Phase D3 is the first authority-expanding step and this keeps the new bridge surface cleanly separable from later bounded tasks.

Lifecycle documentation confirmed mostly clear; one point flagged as worth crisping later (whether canonical handling of a completed request is move-to-processed/rejected, an in-file status update, or both) -- not a blocker, the reviewer would not fail the task over it.
evidence_reviewed: schemas/request.schema.json (full file, read via remote repo access)
risks: Disclosed, non-blocking: created_at/request_id/source are unconstrained strings and payload is an unconstrained object -- acceptable for a foundation contract per the worker's own stated deferral, revisit if/when a real producer/consumer surfaces a concrete need.

Disclosed, non-blocking: the lifecycle text leaves slightly open whether a processed/rejected request is handled by file move, in-file status update, or both -- worth crisping in a later task, not now.
Disclosed, non-blocking: doctor.ps1 now WARNs on the new .cockpit/request/ directory as unexpected, since that script was intentionally left untouched this cycle -- real but harmless cleanup debt.

This verification was performed via the ChatGPT manual bridge (DECISION-086) with remote, read-only repo access only -- no local execution, no independent re-run of scripts/verify-handback-guardrails.ps1 or other local guardrails. Per docs/VERIFICATION_RESULT_SPEC.md, this is additive review, not a substitute for local-guardrail-based independent verification.
summary: The request-file inbox contract (schema, directory, lifecycle documentation) is sound enough for a foundation task, with only minor, disclosed, non-blocking looseness in field constraints and one lifecycle ambiguity worth revisiting later. Sequencing it ahead of any producer/consumer was independently judged the right call for the first authority-expanding phase. No blocker found.
next_action: Mark task complete. Next Phase D3 task: pcc-pathD-008 (rollover/handoff controls -- the first producer and consumer pair), per docs/PATH_A_PLAN.md section 6.
